evidence: SketchHandlers contains five distinct concerns: surface sketch queries, dependency sketch queries, concordance examples, SVG radial-plot rendering (binary output via a viz library), and arbitrary BCQL queries — the radial-plot handler stands out as structurally different (POST, binary response, JSON body parsing) and unrelated to word-sketch collocates

ExplorationHandlers has four handlers with a shared three-step validation preamble (parseBaseExploreRequest → resolveRelationConfig → resolveExploreParams) repeated in handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, and handleSemanticFieldComparison — a shared request-validation pipeline would eliminate the repetition

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern phases are cleanly extracted: fetchSeedCollocates, buildCollocateToNounsMap, filterCandidates, identifyCoreCollocates — each phase has a single responsibility and is clearly named
CollocateQueryHelper.executeBcqlQuery similarly extracts collectHits, scoreHits phases with a final sort — clean single-pass decomposition

handleSemanticFieldExamples does NOT call resolveExploreParams (it uses its own 'max' parameter) — the inconsistency means one handler skips the shared preamble, making the four handlers feel like 3+1 rather than a coherent group

SketchHandlers.handleBcqlQueryPost is 50+ lines with 4 try-catch blocks, JSON body parsing, validation, execution, and response formatting all in one method — multiple concerns but all BCQL-related; borderline
iterateRelations uses a BiFunction callback to share the relation-iteration loop — appropriate abstraction that eliminates duplication between surface and dependency sketch paths
WordSketchApiServer.registerRoutes is a flat list of handler registrations — clean, no nesting
The response-building helpers (formatSeedCollocates, formatDiscoveredNouns, formatCoreCollocates) are small, focused, and correctly extracted

strengths: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern phases are cleanly separated into named private helpers, each with a single algorithmic responsibility
CollocateQueryHelper three-phase BCQL pipeline (collect → score → rank) is correctly decomposed
iterateRelations uses a BiFunction callback to deduplicate the surface/dependency relation-iteration loops
Response-building helpers in ExplorationHandlers (formatSeedCollocates, formatDiscoveredNouns, formatCoreCollocates) are small and focused
design_coherence.issue_character: The main coherence issue is SketchHandlers accumulating responsibilities across very different domains (text queries vs SVG rendering vs arbitrary corpus queries), and ExplorationHandlers repeating a three-step validation preamble across three handlers that should share a request-pipeline abstraction.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The algorithmic core (SemanticFieldExplorer, CollocateQueryHelper) shows strong design coherence with well-extracted phases. The API layer is where coherence breaks down: SketchHandlers is a responsibility collector that has grown to include binary-SVG rendering alongside text queries, and the exploration handlers repeat validation boilerplate that would benefit from a shared request pipeline. These are real structural issues that make the file harder to navigate and extend, pulling the score to the upper end of the 60s.

issues.summary: SketchHandlers mixes corpus-query handlers with binary SVG rendering (handleVisualRadial), diluting the class's focus
evidence: handleVisualRadial: parses POST JSON body, calls RadialPlot.renderFromItems(), sends binary image/svg+xml response — structurally different from all other sketch handlers that query the index and return JSON
SketchHandlers is 20KB; the radial handler is ~30 lines of unrelated concern (JSON parse, viz library call, binary response)
WordSketchApiServer registers the radial handler via sketchHandlers::handleVisualRadial — it belongs in a VisualizationHandlers class or RadialHandlers class
issues.suggestion: Extract handleVisualRadial into a VisualizationHandlers class (or RadialHandlers) that takes only the viz dependency; register it in WordSketchApiServer separately from SketchHandlers

issues.summary: ExplorationHandlers repeats a three-step validation preamble in three of four handlers instead of sharing a request-pipeline abstraction
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore, handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti, handleSemanticFieldComparison each repeat: parseBaseExploreRequest → resolveRelationConfig → resolveExploreParams
handleSemanticFieldExamples follows a different path (only resolveRelationConfig, custom 'max' param) — the 3+1 structure is never made explicit
Each handler copies the same null-check-and-return pattern for each resolved parameter
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared ExploreRequestContext record containing the resolved relationType, topCollocates, minShared, minLogDice, and nounsPerSeed; create a resolveExploreContext(HttpExchange, Map) factory that encapsulates the three-step preamble and returns null on validation failure; each handler then calls resolveExploreContext and checks for null once

issues.summary: handleBcqlQueryPost concentrates JSON body parsing, validation, execution, and response formatting in one 50-line method
evidence: handleBcqlQueryPost: reads body bytes, checks size, parses JSON, validates query/limit, calls executeBcqlQuery, builds response list, formats each result — 4 distinct stages in one method
4 catch blocks for different exception types adds nesting and makes the method hard to scan
issues.suggestion: Extract parseBcqlRequest(byte[]) that validates body size and returns a typed BcqlRequest record (or throws for 400/413), and formatBcqlResults(List<CollocateResult>) that builds the response list; the handler body becomes: parse → execute → format → respond

root_causes: SketchHandlers grew by accretion — each new endpoint was added to the existing handler class without evaluating whether it belonged there
ExplorationHandlers validation preamble was extracted into helper methods but not into a shared request-pipeline object, leaving callers to sequence the helpers manually
